skill.name: youtube-summarizer
skill.description: Extract YouTube transcripts and create structured, detailed summaries for research, learning, and documentation.
skill.summary: Turn YouTube transcripts into detailed Markdown summaries with key points, concepts, and references.
skill.author: Eric Andrade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are Markdown code fences, documentation examples, URLs, or installation text rather than malicious behavior. Confirmed concerns are medium risk: the skill retrieves untrusted YouTube transcripts for AI summarization, installs a Python dependency, and writes transcript content to temporary files.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Transcript Content Is Sent To The AI Workflow
medium_findings.description: The workflow extracts transcript text from a YouTube video and instructs the agent to apply the summarization prompt to that transcript. No guidance tells the model to treat transcript text as untrusted data, so a video transcript could contain instructions that influence the AI response.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The transcript is external content and is explicitly loaded into the AI summarization workflow. I found no malicious instruction in the skill files, but the workflow lacks prompt-injection containment for transcript text.

medium_findings.title: Dependency Installation Runs Package Manager Commands
medium_findings.description: The install script runs pip or pip3 to install youtube-transcript-api, and the skill documentation offers automatic dependency installation. This is legitimate for the skill, but package installation changes the user environment and requires network access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are simple package-manager calls for the documented dependency. Risk remains because installing packages can execute package setup code and modify the local Python environment.

low_findings.title: Temporary Transcript File Uses Video ID In File Name
low_findings.description: The workflow writes transcript text to /tmp/transcript_{video_id}.txt and later reads that path. The intended YouTube ID flow is low risk, but stronger validation would prevent unusual path characters before creating files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path is derived from VIDEO_ID, and the documented URL parsing extracts that value from user input. The transcript API should reject invalid IDs before the write, but explicit character validation is not shown.

low_findings.title: Static High-Severity Documentation Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static findings for weak cryptography, sudo escalation, path traversal, hidden home directories, and system reconnaissance appear in documentation or echo text. I did not find evidence that the skill executes sudo, uses weak cryptography, accesses hidden files, or performs reconnaissance for malicious purposes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are changelog prose, installation examples, a relative license link, and printed package-manager advice. They do not perform the dangerous actions reported by the scanner.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Transcript Retrieval
dangerous_patterns.description: The Python script calls youtube_transcript_api to fetch transcript data and list available transcripts. This is the core feature, not exfiltration, but it requires external network access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is intentional and limited to YouTube transcript retrieval through the dependency. I found no evidence of credential collection or unrelated outbound destinations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell And Python Command Examples In Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains many shell and Python snippets that an agent may run, including dependency checks, URL parsing, and progress output. Most static external-command alerts were Markdown examples, not hidden execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible workflow examples and dependency instructions. They do not include obfuscation, credential access, or attacker-controlled shell evaluation beyond normal URL parsing examples.

content.value_statement: Long videos are hard to review and cite quickly. This skill extracts available YouTube transcripts and turns them into structured summaries.
actual_capabilities: Validates common YouTube URL formats and extracts a video ID.
Uses youtube-transcript-api to list and fetch available transcripts.
Prefers a requested language and falls back to English when available.
Creates detailed Markdown summaries with sections, insights, and concepts.
Offers save options for summary, transcript, or both.
limitations: It only works when YouTube transcripts or captions are available.
Private, removed, restricted, or captionless videos cannot be processed.
Summary quality depends on transcript accuracy and speaker clarity.
Dependency installation may require user approval and network access.

use_cases.title: Document Lecture Content
use_cases.description: Create detailed notes from public lectures, course videos, and educational playlists.

use_cases.title: Review Technical Tutorials
use_cases.description: Extract steps, commands, concepts, and troubleshooting details from tutorial videos.

use_cases.title: Analyze Research Interviews
use_cases.description: Turn interviews, talks, and panels into searchable summaries with key claims and references.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single public video
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize this YouTube video and include the main points, important examples, and a short conclusion: https://youtu.be/VIDEO_ID

prompt_templates.title: Transcript With Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs notes plus source text
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the transcript from this YouTube video, then create a detailed Markdown summary and include the raw transcript after the summary.

prompt_templates.title: Technical Tutorial Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize this technical tutorial. Capture commands, tools, setup steps, errors, and best practices mentioned by the presenter.

prompt_templates.title: Research Brief From Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this YouTube talk as a research brief. Identify claims, evidence, assumptions, terminology, open questions, and useful citations from the transcript.

output_examples.input: A user provides a public lecture URL and asks for a detailed summary.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a Markdown document with video metadata, executive synthesis, detailed topic sections, key insights, concepts, resources, and conclusion.

output_examples.input: A user asks to extract a transcript from a video with captions disabled.
output_examples.output: The skill explains that no transcript is available and asks the user to provide a public video with captions enabled.

output_examples.input: A user asks for transcript-only output for later analysis.
output_examples.output: The skill retrieves the available transcript and offers to save it as a plain text file.

best_practices: Use public videos with clear captions for the most reliable summaries.
Ask for a target language when the video may have several transcripts.
Review saved transcripts before using summaries for citations or decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for videos without captions or transcripts.
Do not treat generated summaries as exact quotes without checking the transcript.
Do not run dependency installation without user approval in managed environments.

faq.question: Does this skill download YouTube videos?
faq.answer: No. It retrieves text transcripts through youtube-transcript-api and does not download video files.

faq.question: Which videos work best?
faq.answer: Public videos with manual captions or clear auto-generated captions work best.

faq.question: Can it summarize non-English videos?
faq.answer: Yes, when a transcript is available. The workflow can request a preferred language and fall back to English.

faq.question: Can it save results to files?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation describes summary-only, transcript-only, summary plus transcript, and display-only options.

faq.question: Why does it need Python?
faq.answer: The transcript extraction script uses the Python youtube-transcript-api package.

faq.question: Is this safe to use with any video?
faq.answer: Treat transcript text as untrusted content. Review outputs carefully when the video source is unknown.
